reasoning: I'm voting INC because at least it represents what I actually believe in—constitutional secularism, the idea that citizenship shouldn't be based on religion, the principle that institutions should work for everyone. I'm not doing this because I think they'll win Bangaon or that they'll change anything for me. I'm doing it because after three years of watching both TMC and BJP play the same patronage game with different symbols, after the WBSSC scam confirmed that even the exam system I've been grinding away at is rigged, I need to at least vote for the values I actually hold instead of against the side I hate slightly less. Shantanu Thakur will probably win. Biswajit Das will probably lose. Nothing will change for jobs or the exam system. But I can't vote for either of them knowing what I know, so I'm voting for what I believe in, even if it's pointless.
primary_drivers: rejection of both major-party patronage machines
the WBSSC scam breaking his faith in state institutions entirely
